author: Sofia Martínez
title: How Roofing Companies Win $8K-$12K Jobs with Google Local Services Ads
description: Stop competing on price. We show roofing companies how to dominate LSA, convert emergency calls faster, and reduce cost per qualified lead by 40%.

sections.p: We've watched roofing companies lose 30% of their leads to competitors who answered the phone 90 seconds faster. That's not a sales problem—that's a system problem. Google Local Services Ads puts your business in front of homeowners actively searching "emergency roof repair" or "roof replacement near me," but only if you're set up to respond immediately and close the job. We're going to walk you through the exact setup most roofing companies miss, the bid strategy that actually wins jobs under $50K, and how to track which lead sources actually convert to signed contracts.

sections.h2: Why Google LSA Beats Google Ads for Roofing

sections.p: Google Local Services Ads charges you only when someone calls or messages—not per click. For roofing, this is critical. A homeowner searching "roof leak repair" isn't browsing; they're panicking. They call the first contractor who answers. LSA campaigns spend 35-50% less per qualified lead than traditional Google Ads because you're not paying for clicks from people just comparing prices.

You'll see your company name, phone number, and Google Guaranteed badge front and center—above paid search results. You can set service areas down to ZIP code level. If you operate in three counties, you only pay for calls from those areas. Most roofing companies running LSA report 18-24 calls per week at $45-$85 cost per call. That's $810-$2040 weekly for qualified, local leads—most of which come from storm damage, urgent repairs, and seasonal demand.

sections.h2: The Setup Most Roofers Get Wrong

list: Missing or incomplete Google Business Profile—no service areas defined, no photos of actual roof work, no response to messages within 1 hour
Not separating emergency repair calls from routine maintenance inquiries—you need different pricing tiers, so bids reflect urgency
Bid strategy set too low—underbidding because you don't track which call-to-estimate conversion rates actually matter
No call tracking—you can't tell if calls from LSA convert at 25% or 5%, so you keep overspending on dead-end calls
Ignoring seasonal demand—not increasing bids during storm season (spring/fall in most regions) when homeowners are actively searching

sections.h2: How to Set Bid Strategy for Roofing Jobs

sections.p: Your LSA bid controls how prominently your company appears. Don't set it flat. We recommend this: Emergency repairs (roof leaks, storm damage) $65-$95 per call. Seasonal inspections $35-$50. Replacements $50-$75. Why? Emergency calls convert at 55-65% because the homeowner has an urgent problem. Seasonal calls convert at 25-35% because they're shopping around. Your bid should reflect conversion probability, not just traffic volume.

Track every call for 30 days. Use call recording (with legal consent) to audit which calls led to estimates, which converted to signed contracts, and which fell apart at objection stage. Most roofing companies we've worked with discover that 40% of their "qualified" calls are from senior citizens or renters who can't authorize the work. Adjust your bid down during those seasons and redirect budget to homeowner-focused keywords.

sections.h2: The Conversion Funnel That Closes Jobs

sections.quote: You need to answer within 90 seconds, qualify the caller in 60 seconds, and send an estimate request link within 5 minutes. If you do those three things, you'll convert 40% more calls into jobs.

sections.p: After the call: Send an SMS with your estimated arrival window and a link to pre-qualify the roof type and damage photos. This does two things. First, it proves you're organized and professional. Second, you get visual proof of the damage before you even dispatch a crew, which speeds up estimating on-site. Within 24 hours of the site visit, send a detailed written estimate via email (PDF, itemized, includes materials, timeline, warranty). Roofing is a high-consideration purchase—homeowners compare 2-3 estimates before signing. A detailed estimate with photos and warranty terms closes 35% faster than a verbal quote.
